records.chapter: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5345 - Wuhayb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a boy who entered the house of some people to play and fell into their well. Are they liable?
Imam (as) said: "They are not liable, but if they are accused (of negligence or wrongdoing), then they are liable."
records.arabicText: 5345 - وَ رَوَى وُهَيْبُ بْنُ حَفْصٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ غُلاَمٍ دَخَلَ دَارَ قَوْمٍ يَلْعَبُ فَوَقَعَ فِي بِئْرِهِمْ أَ يَضْمَنُونَ قَالَ "لَيْسَ يَضْمَنُونَ وَ إِنْ كَانُوا مُتَّهَمِينَ ضَمِنُوا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5345 - Wuhayb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a boy who entered the house of some people to play and fell into their well. Are they liable?
Imam (as) said: "They are not liable, but if they are accused (of negligence or wrongdoing), then they are liable."

records.chapter: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5346 - Al-Husayn ibn Said narrated from Ali ibn Al-Numan, who narrated from Abu Al-Sabbah Al-Kinani, who narrates that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"Whoever causes harm to any part of the path of the Muslims is liable for it."
records.arabicText: 5346 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلنُّعْمَانِ عَنْ أَبِي اَلصَّبَّاحِ اَلْكِنَانِيِّ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَنْ أَضَرَّ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ طَرِيقِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ فَهُوَ لَهُ ضَامِنٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5346 - Al-Husayn ibn Said narrated from Ali ibn Al-Numan, who narrated from Abu Al-Sabbah Al-Kinani, who narrates that Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever causes harm to any part of the path of the Muslims is liable for it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5347 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about an object placed on the road, and an animal passes by it, becomes startled, and injures its rider.
Imam (as) said: "Anything that causes harm on the path of the Muslims, its owner is liable for whatever results from it."
records.arabicText: 5347 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلشَّيْءِ يُوضَعُ عَلَى اَلطَّرِيقِ فَتَمُرُّ بِهِ اَلدَّابَّةُ فَتَنْفِرُ بِصَاحِبِهَا فَتَعْقِرُهُ قَالَ "كُلُّ شَيْءٍ يُضِرُّ بِطَرِيقِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ فَصَاحِبُهُ ضَامِنٌ لِمَا يُصِيبُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5347 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about an object placed on the road, and an animal passes by it, becomes startled, and injures its rider.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Anything that causes harm on the path of the Muslims, its owner is liable for whatever results from it."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory When an Animal Strikes a Person with Its Front or Hind Legs
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5348 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about a man passing through one of the paths of the Muslims, and his animal strikes a person with its leg.
Imam (as) said: "He is not liable for what it strikes with its leg, but he is liable for what it strikes with its front legs because its hind leg is behind him if he is riding. However, if he is leading the animal, then by the permission of Allah (swt), he has control over its front legs and can place them wherever he wishes."
records.arabicText: 5348 - رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَمُرُّ عَلَى طَرِيقٍ مِنْ طُرُقِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ فَتُصِيبُ دَابَّتُهُ إِنْسَاناً بِرِجْلِهَا فَقَالَ "لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ مَا أَصَابَتْ بِرِجْلِهَا وَ لَكِنْ عَلَيْهِ مَا أَصَابَتْ بِيَدَيْهَا لِأَنَّ رِجْلَهَا خَلْفَهُ إِنْ رَكِبَ وَ إِنْ قَادَ دَابَّتَهُ فَإِنَّهُ يَمْلِكُ بِإِذْنِ اَللَّهِ يَدَيْهَا يَضَعُهُمَا حَيْثُ يَشَاءُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5348 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he was asked about a man passing through one of the paths of the Muslims, and his animal strikes a person with its leg.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He is not liable for what it strikes with its leg, but he is liable for what it strikes with its front legs because its hind leg is behind him if he is riding. However, if he is leading the animal, then by the permission of Allah (swt), he has control over its front legs and can place them wherever he wishes."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory When an Animal Strikes a Person with Its Front or Hind Legs
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5349 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, who narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) regarding a man who placed his servant on an animal, and the animal trampled a man.
Imam (as) said: "The compensation is upon the master."
records.arabicText: 5349 - وَ رَوَى الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع‌ فِي رَجُلٍ حَمَلَ عَبْدَهُ عَلَى دَابَّةٍ فَوَطِئَتْ رَجُلًا فَقَالَ الْغُرْمُ عَلَى مَوْلَاهُ‌.

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory When an Animal Strikes a Person with Its Front or Hind Legs
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5350 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated, raising it to Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"The owners of livestock are not liable for anything as long as the animals are left to roam freely."
records.arabicText: 5350 - وَ رَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ رَفَعَهُ إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "بَهِيمَةُ اَلْأَنْعَامِ لاَ يَغْرَمُ أَهْلُهَا شَيْئاً مَا دَامَتْ مُرْسَلَةً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5350 - Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated, raising it to Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The owners of livestock are not liable for anything as long as the animals are left to roam freely."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory When an Animal Strikes a Person with Its Front or Hind Legs
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5351 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni:
"Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to hold the one leading (the animal), the one driving (the animal), and the rider accountable."
records.arabicText: 5351 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَانَ يُضَمِّنُ اَلْقَائِدَ وَ اَلسَّائِقَ وَ اَلرَّاكِبَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5351 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to hold the one leading (the animal), the one driving (the animal), and the rider accountable."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory When an Animal Strikes a Person with Its Front or Hind Legs
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5352 - "And the Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled regarding an animal carrying two riders, and the animal killed or injured a man.
Imam (as) ruled that the compensation would be equally divided between the two riders."
records.arabicText: 5352 - : "وَ قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي دَابَّةٍ عَلَيْهَا رَدِيفَانِ فَقَتَلَتِ اَلدَّابَّةُ رَجُلاً أَوْ جَرَحَتْهُ فَقَضَى بِالْغَرَامَةِ بَيْنَ اَلرَّدِيفَيْنِ بِالسَّوِيَّةِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory When an Animal Strikes a Person with Its Front or Hind Legs
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5353 - In the narration of Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) held the owner of the animal liable for what it trampled with its front legs and what it struck with its hind legs. However, there is no liability upon him unless a person strikes the animal."
records.arabicText: 5353 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ غِيَاثِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ضَمَّنَ صَاحِبَ اَلدَّابَّةِ مَا وَطِئَتْ بِيَدَيْهَا وَ مَا نَفَحَتْ بِرِجْلَيْهَا فَلاَ ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَضْرِبَهَا إِنْسَانٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5353 - In the narration of Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) held the owner of the animal liable for what it trampled with its front legs and what it struck with its hind legs. However, there is no liability upon him unless a person strikes the animal."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding Two Men Who Together Sever the Hand of a Man
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5354 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, who narrated from Abu Maryam Al-Ansari, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding two men who together cut off the hand of another man.
Imam (as) said: "If the victim wishes to cut off their hands, he must first pay them the compensation for one hand, which they will then divide between themselves, and afterward, he may cut off both of their hands.
However, if he wishes, he may take the compensation for his hand from them. If he cuts off the hand of only one of them, then the one whose hand was not cut off must pay a quarter of the compensation to the one whose hand was cut off."
records.arabicText: 5354 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ اَلْأَنْصَارِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلَيْنِ اِجْتَمَعَا عَلَى قَطْعِ يَدِ رَجُلٍ فَقَالَ "إِنْ أَحَبَّ أَنْ يَقْطَعَهُمَا أَدَّى إِلَيْهِمَا دِيَةَ يَدٍ فَاقْتَسَمَاهَا ثُمَّ يَقْطَعُهُمَا وَ إِنْ أَحَبَّ أَخَذَ مِنْهُمَا دِيَةَ يَدِهِ فَإِنْ قَطَعَ يَدَ أَحَدِهِمَا رَدَّ اَلَّذِي لَمْ تُقْطَعْ يَدُهُ عَلَى اَلَّذِي قُطِعَتْ يَدُهُ رُبُعَ اَلدِّيَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5354 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, who narrated from Abu Maryam Al-Ansari, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding two men who together cut off the hand of another man.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "If the victim wishes to cut off their hands, he must first pay them the compensation for one hand, which they will then divide between themselves, and afterward, he may cut off both of their hands.
However, if he wishes, he may take the compensation for his hand from them. If he cuts off the hand of only one of them, then the one whose hand was not cut off must pay a quarter of the compensation to the one whose hand was cut off."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Severes the Head of a Deceased Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5355 - Al-Husayn ibn Khalid narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
"The diyyah (blood money) for a fetus, if its mother is struck and it is miscarried before the soul is breathed into it, is one hundred dinars, and this belongs to its heirs.
As for the diyyah of a deceased person whose head is severed and whose abdomen is cut open, it does not go to his heirs; rather, it belongs to him alone."
I asked: "What is the difference between the two cases?"
Imam (as) said: "The fetus represents a future life, and its benefit is anticipated. But the deceased has already passed away, and his benefit has ceased. Therefore, when he is mutilated after death, the diyyah for the mutilation is his alone, not for others. It is to be used to perform Hajj on his behalf or for acts of charity and other good deeds."
I then asked: "What if a man enters to dig a grave to wash the deceased, but while digging, he loses focus, and his shovel slips from his hand and strikes the abdomen of the deceased, cutting it open? What is required of him?"
Imam (as) said: "If it happened in this manner, it is considered unintentional (khata'), and he is required to perform kaffarah (expiation): to free a slave, or fast for two consecutive months, or feed sixty poor people, giving each one a mudd (a measure) according to the mudd of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family."
records.arabicText: 5355 - رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "دِيَةُ اَلْجَنِينِ إِذَا ضُرِبَتْ أُمُّهُ فَسَقَطَ مِنْ بَطْنِهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ تُنْشَأَ فِيهِ اَلرُّوحُ مِائَةُ دِينَارٍ وَ هِيَ لِوَرَثَتِهِ وَ دِيَةُ اَلْمَيِّتِ إِذَا قُطِعَ رَأْسُهُ وَ شُقَّ بَطْنُهُ فَلَيْسَتْ هِيَ لِوَرَثَتِهِ إِنَّمَا هِيَ لَهُ دُونَ اَلْوَرَثَةِ" فَقُلْتُ وَ مَا اَلْفَرْقُ بَيْنَهُمَا فَقَالَ "إِنَّ اَلْجَنِينَ أَمْرٌ مُسْتَقْبِلٌ يُرْجَى نَفْعُهُ وَ إِنَّ هَذَا قَدْ مَضَى وَ ذَهَبَتْ مَنْفَعَتُهُ فَلَمَّا مُثِّلَ بِهِ بَعْدَ وَفَاتِهِ صَارَتْ دِيَةُ اَلْمُثْلَةِ لَهُ لاَ لِغَيْرِهِ يُحَجُّ بِهَا عَنْهُ أَوْ يُفْعَلُ بِهَا أَبْوَابُ اَلْبِرِّ مِنْ صَدَقَةٍ وَ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ " قُلْتُ فَإِنَّهُ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ رَجُلٌ لِيَحْفِرَ لَهُ بِئْراً يُغَسِّلُهُ فِيهَا فَسَدِرَ اَلرَّجُلُ فِيمَا يَحْفِرُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَمَالَتْ مِسْحَاتُهُ فِي يَدِهِ فَأَصَابَتْ بَطْنَهُ فَشَقَّتْهُ فَمَا عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ هَكَذَا فَهُوَ خَطَأٌ وَ إِنَّمَا عَلَيْهِ اَلْكَفَّارَةُ عِتْقُ رَقَبَةٍ أَوْ صِيَامُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ أَوْ صَدَقَةٌ عَلَى سِتِّينَ مِسْكِيناً مُدٌّ لِكُلِّ مِسْكِينٍ بِمُدِّ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5355 - Al-Husayn ibn Khalid narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The diyyah (blood money) for a fetus, if its mother is struck and it is miscarried before the soul is breathed into it, is one hundred dinars, and this belongs to its heirs.
As for the diyyah of a deceased person whose head is severed and whose abdomen is cut open, it does not go to his heirs; rather, it belongs to him alone."
I asked: "What is the difference between the two cases?"
Imam (as) said: "The fetus represents a future life, and its benefit is anticipated. But the deceased has already passed away, and his benefit has ceased. Therefore, when he is mutilated after death, the diyyah for the mutilation is his alone, not for others. It is to be used to perform Hajj on his behalf or for acts of charity and other good deeds."
I then asked: "What if a man enters to dig a grave to wash the deceased, but while digging, he loses focus, and his shovel slips from his hand and strikes the abdomen of the deceased, cutting it open? What is required of him?"
Imam (as) said: "If it happened in this manner, it is considered unintentional (khata'), and he is required to perform kaffarah (expiation): to free a slave, or fast for two consecutive months, or feed sixty poor people, giving each one a mudd (a measure) according to the mudd of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Severes the Head of a Deceased Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5356 - In Nawadir of Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr, it is narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: "Beheading the dead is more severe than beheading the living."
records.arabicText: 5356 - وَ فِي نَوَادِرِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ أَنَّ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "قَطْعُ رَأْسِ اَلْمَيِّتِ أَشَدُّ مِنْ قَطْعِ رَأْسِ اَلْحَيِّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5356 - In Nawadir of Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr, it is narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Beheading the dead is more severe than beheading the living."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Severes the Head of a Deceased Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5357 - In the narration of Abdullah ibn Muskan from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), regarding a man who beheaded a dead person
Imam (as) said: "The blood money is upon him because the sanctity of the deceased is like his sanctity when he was alive."
records.arabicText: 5357 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَطَعَ رَأْسَ اَلْمَيِّتِ قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ اَلدِّيَةُ لِأَنَّ حُرْمَتَهُ مَيِّتاً كَحُرْمَتِهِ وَ هُوَ حَيٌّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5357 - In the narration of Abdullah ibn Muskan from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), regarding a man who beheaded a dead person
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The blood money is upon him because the sanctity of the deceased is like his sanctity when he was alive."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Severes the Head of a Deceased Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5358 - It is narrated from Abu Jamilah, from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a deceased person whose head was cut off.
Imam (as) said: "The blood money is upon the one who did it."
I asked: "Who receives his blood money?"
Imam (as) said: "The Imam, for this belongs to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic. And if his right hand or any of his limbs is cut off, the compensation (Al-arsh) is also due to the Imam."
records.arabicText: 5358 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي جَمِيلَةَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَيِّتٌ قُطِعَ رَأْسُهُ قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ اَلدِّيَةُ" قُلْتُ فَمَنْ يَأْخُذُ دِيَتَهُ قَالَ "اَلْإِمَامُ هَذَا لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ إِنْ قُطِعَتْ يَمِينُهُ أَوْ شَيْءٌ مِنْ جَوَارِحِهِ فَعَلَيْهِ اَلْأَرْشُ لِلْإِمَامِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5358 - It is narrated from Abu Jamilah, from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a deceased person whose head was cut off.
Imam (as) said: "The blood money is upon the one who did it."
I asked: "Who receives his blood money?"
Imam (as) said: "The Imam, for this belongs to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic. And if his right hand or any of his limbs is cut off, the compensation (Al-arsh) is also due to the Imam."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Slap That Causes Blackening, Greening, or Reddening
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5359 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who slapped another man on his face, and the slap caused the skin to turn black.
Imam (as) said: "If the slap causes the skin to turn black, the compensation is six dinars. If it turns green, the compensation is three dinars. If it turns red, the compensation is one and a half dinars. For the same injuries on the body, the compensation is half of that amount."
records.arabicText: 5359 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ لَطَمَ رَجُلاً عَلَى وَجْهِهِ فَاسْوَدَّتِ اَللَّطْمَةُ فَقَالَ "إِذَا اِسْوَدَّتِ اَللَّطْمَةُ فَفِيهَا سِتَّةُ دَنَانِيرَ وَ إِذَا اِخْضَرَّتْ فَفِيهَا ثَلاَثَةُ دَنَانِيرَ وَ إِذَا اِحْمَرَّتْ فَفِيهَا دِينَارٌ وَ نِصْفٌ وَ فِي اَلْبَدَنِ نِصْفُ ذَلِكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5359 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who slapped another man on his face, and the slap caused the skin to turn black.
Imam (as) said: "If the slap causes the skin to turn black, the compensation is six dinars. If it turns green, the compensation is three dinars. If it turns red, the compensation is one and a half dinars. For the same injuries on the body, the compensation is half of that amount."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Approaches a Sleeping Man, and When He Turns on His Back, He Awakens and Kills Him
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5360 - Al-Husayn ibn Khalid narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Al-Awwal (as) that he was asked about a man who approached another man while he was lying down. When he got on top of him, the man woke up and struck him with a severe blow, killing him.
Imam (as) said: "There is neither blood money (compensation) for him nor retaliation (legal retribution)."
records.arabicText: 5360 - رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَتَى رَجُلاً وَ هُوَ رَاقِدٌ فَلَمَّا صَارَ عَلَى ظَهْرِهِ اِنْتَبَهَ فَبَعَجَهُ بَعْجَةً فَقَتَلَهُ قَالَ "لاَ دِيَةَ لَهُ وَ لاَ قَوَدَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5360 - Al-Husayn ibn Khalid narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Al-Awwal (as) that he was asked about a man who approached another man while he was lying down. When he got on top of him, the man woke up and struck him with a severe blow, killing him.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is neither blood money (compensation) for him nor retaliation (legal retribution)."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding Three People Who Together Demolish a Wall, and it Falls on One of Them, Causing His Death
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5361 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, who narrated from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"The Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled regarding the demolition of a wall in which three people participated. When it collapsed on one of them and caused his death, he held the remaining two responsible for his blood money because each one of them is accountable for his companion."
records.arabicText: 5361 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي هَدْمِ حَائِطٍ اِشْتَرَكَ فِيهِ ثَلاَثَةٌ فَوَقَعَ عَلَى وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمْ فَمَاتَ فَضَمَّنَ اَلْبَاقِينَ دِيَتَهُ، لِأَنَّ كُلَّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمْ ضَامِنُ صَاحِبِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5361 - Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, who narrated from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled regarding the demolition of a wall in which three people participated. When it collapsed on one of them and caused his death, he held the remaining two responsible for his blood money because each one of them is accountable for his companion."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who is Killed While He Has an Outstanding Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5362 - Muhammad ibn Aslam Al-Jabali narrated from Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman, who narrated from Abdullah ibn Muskan, who narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who is killed while he has debt and possesses no wealth. Do his heirs have the right to pardon his killer while he has debt?
Imam (as) said: "The creditors are the ones who have a claim against the killer. If his heirs pardon his killer, they become responsible for paying the debt to the creditors. If they do not pardon him, then they are not responsible."
records.arabicText: 5362 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ اَلْجَبَلِيُّ عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يُقْتَلُ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ وَ لَيْسَ لَهُ مَالٌ فَهَلْ لِأَوْلِيَائِهِ أَنْ يَهَبُوا دَمَهُ لِقَاتِلِهِ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ فَقَالَ "إِنَّ أَصْحَابَ اَلدَّيْنِ هُمُ اَلْخُصَمَاءُ لِلْقَاتِلِ فَإِنْ وَهَبَ أَوْلِيَاؤُهُ دَمَهُ لِلْقَاتِلِ ضَمِنُوا اَلدَّيْنَ لِلْغُرَمَاءِ وَ إِلاَّ فَلاَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5362 - Muhammad ibn Aslam Al-Jabali narrated from Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman, who narrated from Abdullah ibn Muskan, who narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who is killed while he has debt and possesses no wealth. Do his heirs have the right to pardon his killer while he has debt?
Imam (as) said: "The creditors are the ones who have a claim against the killer. If his heirs pardon his killer, they become responsible for paying the debt to the creditors. If they do not pardon him, then they are not responsible."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of a Wet Nurse if She Rolls Over a Child, Causing Death, or Hands the Child Over to Another Wet Nurse Who Then Disappears with the Child
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5363 - Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya ibn Imran Al-Ash'ari narrated from Muhammad ibn Najiyah, who narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali, who narrated from Abd Al-Rahman ibn Salim, who narrated from his father, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"Any wet nurse of a people who accidentally kills their child while sleeping - by rolling over and causing the child's death - the blood money is to be paid from her personal wealth if she took on nursing for the sake of pride and honor.
However, if she undertook nursing due to poverty, then the blood money is upon her family ('Aqilah)."
records.arabicText:
5363 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ عِمْرَانَ اَلْأَشْعَرِيُّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ نَاجِيَةَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ:
"أَيُّمَا ظِئْرِ قَوْمٍ قَتَلَتْ صَبِيّاً لَهُمْ وَ هِيَ نَائِمَةٌ فَانْقَلَبَتْ عَلَيْهِ فَقَتَلَتْهُ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْهَا اَلدِّيَةُ مِنْ مَالِهَا خَاصَّةً إِنْ كَانَتْ إِنَّمَا ظَاءَرَتْ طَلَبَ اَلْعِزِّ وَ اَلْفَخْرِ وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ إِنَّمَا ظَاءَرَتْ مِنَ اَلْفَقْرِ فَإِنَّ اَلدِّيَةَ عَلَى عَاقِلَتِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5363 - Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya ibn Imran Al-Ash'ari narrated from Muhammad ibn Najiyah, who narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali, who narrated from Abd Al-Rahman ibn Salim, who narrated from his father, who narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Any wet nurse of a people who accidentally kills their child while sleeping - by rolling over and causing the child's death - the blood money is to be paid from her personal wealth if she took on nursing for the sake of pride and honor.
However, if she undertook nursing due to poverty, then the blood money is upon her family ('Aqilah)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of a Wet Nurse if She Rolls Over a Child, Causing Death, or Hands the Child Over to Another Wet Nurse Who Then Disappears with the Child
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5364 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Sulayman ibn Khalid, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who hired a wet nurse and gave her his child. While the child was with her, the wet nurse went and hired another wet nurse.
The second wet nurse disappeared with the child, and it was unknown what happened to the child, and the original wet nurse could not be held accountable.
Imam (as) said: "The full blood money (diyyah) is due."
records.arabicText:
5364 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اسْتَأْجَرَ ظِئْراً فَأَعْطَاهُ وَلَدَهُ فَكَانَ عِنْدَهَا فَانْطَلَقَتِ الظِّئْرُ فَاسْتَأْجَرَتْ ظِئْراً أُخْرَى فَغَابَتِ الظِّئْرُ بِالْوَلَدِ فَلَا يُدْرَى مَا صُنِعَ بِهِ وَ الظِّئْرُ لَا تُكَافَى قَالَ الدِّيَةُ كَامِلَةً.
وَ رَوَاهُ عَلِيُّ بْنُ النُّعْمَانِ عَنِ ابْنِ مُسْكَانَ‌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع‌ مِثْلَهُ- وَ رَوَاهُ حَمَّادٌ عَنِ الْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع‌ مِثْلَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5364 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Sulayman ibn Khalid, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who hired a wet nurse and gave her his child. While the child was with her, the wet nurse went and hired another wet nurse.
The second wet nurse disappeared with the child, and it was unknown what happened to the child, and the original wet nurse could not be held accountable.
Imam (as) said: "The full blood money (diyyah) is due."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Liability of a Wet Nurse if She Rolls Over a Child, Causing Death, or Hands the Child Over to Another Wet Nurse Who Then Disappears with the Child
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5365 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a man who hired a wet nurse and entrusted his child to her. She disappeared with the child for several years and then returned with the child. The mother claimed that she did not recognize the child.
Imam (as) said: "They do not have the right to reject him; they must accept him, for the wet nurse is trusted."
records.arabicText: 5365 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ: سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِسْتَأْجَرَ ظِئْراً فَدَفَعَ إِلَيْهَا وَلَدَهُ فَغَابَتْ عَنْهُ بِهِ سِنِينَ ثُمَّ جَاءَتْ بِالْوَلَدِ فَزَعَمَتْ أُمُّهُ أَنَّهَا لاَ تَعْرِفُهُ قَالَ "لَيْسَ لَهُمْ ذَلِكَ فَلْيَقْبَلُوهُ فَإِنَّمَا اَلظِّئْرُ مَأْمُونَةٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5365 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a man who hired a wet nurse and entrusted his child to her. She disappeared with the child for several years and then returned with the child. The mother claimed that she did not recognize the child.
Imam (as) said: "They do not have the right to reject him; they must accept him, for the wet nurse is trusted."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Liability is Obligatory Upon the Owner of a Dog if it Bites
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5366 - Al-Husayn ibn Alwan narrated from Amr ibn Khalid, from Zayd ibn Ali, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as):
"Imam (as) used to hold the owner of a dog liable if it bit during the day, but he would not hold him liable if it bit during the night.
And if you enter the house of people with their permission and their dog bites you, they are liable. However, if you enter without their permission, there is no liability upon them."
records.arabicText:
`
5366 - رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ عُلْوَانَ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُضَمِّنُ صَاحِبَ اَلْكَلْبِ إِذَا عَقَرَ نَهَاراً وَ لاَ يُضَمِّنُهُ إِذَا عَقَرَ بِاللَّيْلِ".
وَ إِذَا دَخَلْتَ دَارَ قَوْمٍ بِإِذْنِهِمْ فَعَقَرَكَ كَلْبُهُمْ فَهُمْ ضَامِنُونَ وَ إِذَا دَخَلْتَ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِهِمْ فَلَا ضَمَانَ عَلَيْهِمْ‌.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5366 - Al-Husayn ibn Alwan narrated from Amr ibn Khalid, from Zayd ibn Ali, from his forefathers (peace be upon them), from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Imam (as) used to hold the owner of a dog liable if it bit during the day, but he would not hold him liable if it bit during the night.
And if you enter the house of people with their permission and their dog bites you, they are liable. However, if you enter without their permission, there is no liability upon them."

records.chapter: Techapter 59 - Chapter on Umm Al-Walad Killing Her Master by Mistake or Intentionar
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5367 - Wahb ibn Wahb narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that Imam (as) used to say:
"If a slave woman who has borne a child (umm Al-walad) kills her master by mistake, she becomes free and there is no liability upon her. However, if she intentionally kills him, she is to be executed for him."
records.arabicText: 5367 - رَوَى وَهْبُ بْنُ وَهْبٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: "إِذَا قَتَلَتْ أُمُّ اَلْوَلَدِ سَيِّدَهَا خَطَأً فَهِيَ حُرَّةٌ وَ لاَ تَبِعَةَ عَلَيْهَا وَ إِنْ قَتَلَتْهُ عَمْداً قُتِلَتْ بِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5367 - Wahb ibn Wahb narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that Imam (as) used to say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a slave woman who has borne a child (umm Al-walad) kills her master by mistake, she becomes free and there is no liability upon her. However, if she intentionally kills him, she is to be executed for him."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Sets Fire to the House of a People, Causing the House and Its Inhabitants to Burn
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5368 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni: "Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled regarding a man who came with fire and set it ablaze in the house of some people, causing the house, its inhabitants, and their belongings to burn.
Imam (as) said: 'He must compensate for the value of the house and everything in it, and then he is to be executed.'"
records.arabicText: 5368 - فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَضَى فِي رَجُلٍ أَقْبَلَ بِنَارٍ فَأَشْعَلَهَا فِي دَارِ قَوْمٍ فَاحْتَرَقَتِ اَلدَّارُ وَ اِحْتَرَقَ أَهْلُهَا وَ اِحْتَرَقَ مَتَاعُهُمْ قَالَ "يُغَرَّمُ قِيمَةَ اَلدَّارِ وَ مَا فِيهَا ثُمَّ يُقْتَلُ" ".

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon the Owner of a Frenzied Camel if it Kills a Man
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5369 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), that he was asked about a male camel (bukhti) that became enraged, left the house, and killed a man. Then the brother of the man came and struck the camel with a sword, injuring it.
Imam (as) said: "The owner of the camel is liable for the blood money, and he is entitled to receive the price of his camel."
records.arabicText: 5369 - رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : سُئِلَ عَنْ بُخْتِيٍّ اِغْتَلَمَ فَخَرَجَ مِنَ اَلدَّارِ فَقَتَلَ رَجُلاً فَجَاءَ أَخُو اَلرَّجُلِ فَضَرَبَ اَلْفَحْلَ بِالسَّيْفِ فَعَقَرَهُ فَقَالَ "صَاحِبُ اَلْبُخْتِيِّ ضَامِنٌ لِلدِّيَةِ وَ يَقْبِضُ ثَمَنَ بُخْتِيِّهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5369 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), that he was asked about a male camel (bukhti) that became enraged, left the house, and killed a man. Then the brother of the man came and struck the camel with a sword, injuring it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "The owner of the camel is liable for the blood money, and he is entitled to receive the price of his camel."
